stocks.recent_news: NTPC Green Energy (NGEL) declared COD of 150 MW solar plant in Rajasthan effective 18-Apr-2026, lifting group installed capacity to 89,528 MW. Company opened application window for 250 Assistant Executive (Operations) posts starting 23-Apr-2026 (non-market item). Q3 FY26 EPS Rs 5.36 vs estimate Rs 5.39 (-0.56% surprise); next quarter consensus EPS Rs 6.91. Board meets 15-May-2026 for Q4 FY26 results and final dividend. Stock at ~Rs 402 on 23-Apr-2026, 52W high Rs 430 / 52W low Rs 295. Last dividend Rs 2.75 with ex-date 6-Feb-2026; FY26 total dividends Rs 11.60. Analyst consensus Buy with 12-month target Rs 400-440.
stocks.sector_context: Power sector tactically set up for summer: IMD projects above-normal temps + more heatwave days Mar-May 2026; peak demand forecast 275-285 GW (+8-12% YoY). Extended monsoon dented FY26 generation growth — NTPC output -4% YoY in the prior read; broader thermal utilization subdued. Power stock cohort (NTPC, Adani Power, JSW Energy, Tata Power, NTPC Green, Torrent Power) up 2.7-16% CY26-YTD. 26% US reciprocal tariff announced 2-Apr-2026 is a broad macro overhang via FII outflows and INR volatility. Regulated utilities pegged at 7-8% earnings CAGR FY25-28.
stocks.peer_comparison: NTPC 1-year return ~+16.6% — middle of the power pack (Adani Power +51.5%, Tata Power +0.04%, JSW Energy -4.8%). JM Financial's summer pecking order ranks NTPC #3 behind Adani Power and Tata Power. JM Financial expects NTPC Q4 generation -7% YoY (worst in the cohort on monsoon overhang) vs Adani Power -3%, JSW Energy +37% (acquisition-led). Trading at P/E ~14.9 vs industry ~20.8 — valuation discount to peers persists.
red_flags: Q4 FY26 generation expected -7% YoY per JM Financial — softest in the cohort
US 26% reciprocal tariff macro overhang could pressure FII flows
Stock trading within Rs 2 of top-end analyst consensus target Rs 400-440 — limited near-term upside without a guidance upgrade
Regulated-return model caps tariff-led upside from summer spot shortages
catalysts: Q4 FY26 audited results + final dividend on 15-May-2026 (board meeting confirmed)
Consensus next-quarter EPS Rs 6.91 vs Rs 5.36 last print — >28% QoQ uplift priced in
NGEL further solar/wind COD announcements as FY27 ramp continues
Any broker target upgrade above Rs 440 would be the trigger for a fresh leg

stocks.recent_news: Fresh all-time high Rs 216.50 on 22-Apr-2026 (+6-7% intraday) in a weak tape; market cap crossed Rs 4 lakh cr / ~Rs 4.15 lakh cr. Stock up ~21.5% over 9 sessions, ~40% in the last month, outperforming Sensex's 1.4% rise. Incorporated nuclear step-down subsidiary Rawatbhata-Raj Atomic Energy Limited (RRAEL) on 20-Apr-2026 via Adani Atomic Energy Limited. Q4 FY26 earnings conference call scheduled 29-Apr-2026 at 5:00 PM IST. PPAs cover >90% of operating fleet; largest thermal fleet in India at 18.15 GW (15,250 MW thermal across 8 plants in 7 states). Q3 FY26 PAT was Rs 2,479.58 cr (-18.89% YoY).
stocks.sector_context: Power stocks driven by IMD heatwave forecast, peak demand 275-285 GW, coal-based utilisation expected to stay firm. JM Financial pecking order for summer places Adani Power #1 (above Tata Power, NTPC, Adani Green, Coal India, JSW Energy). Bernstein 'outperform' on Adani Power, Tata Power, JSW Energy. Nifty Next 50 hit a 52-week high led by power + Adani Group names on 22-Apr-2026. CEA flags possible peak demand toward 275 GW (up from 250 GW in 2024).
stocks.peer_comparison: Best-in-cohort 1-year return +51.5% vs NTPC +16.6%, Tata Power ~flat, JSW Energy -4.8%. Top YTD performer in large-cap utilities. JM Financial expects Q4 generation only -3% YoY, materially better than NTPC -7%. 18.15 GW thermal fleet dwarfs next private peer. Valuation extended — RSI overbought, stock 20%+ above 50 DMA after 40% 1-month rally; premium now priced in vs peers.
red_flags: Under BSE Short-term ASM Stage 1 framework since 23-Sep-2025 — active surveillance
Q3 FY26 PAT -18.89% YoY despite strong revenue — margin/cost pressure concerns into Q4
JM Financial expects Q4 generation -3% YoY — modestly negative growth into the 29-Apr print
All-time-high entry with RSI extended; 40% one-month rally increases mean-reversion risk
Adani Group concentration / governance overhang remains a tail risk for FII positioning
Nuclear subsidiary (RRAEL, 20-Apr-2026) is capital-intensive, long-gestation and execution-heavy
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + earnings concall on 29-Apr-2026 — imminent binary event
Nuclear expansion via RRAEL / Adani Atomic Energy Limited — strategic optionality story
Summer peak-demand May-Jun 2026 drives thermal PLF
Bernstein 'outperform' rating and JM Financial #1 summer pick positioning support momentum
>90% PPA coverage gives revenue visibility if management guides firm FY27 PLF
